title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a man giving information about a weekend cycling tour.
audio_script: Man: Welcome to the meeting about this weekend’s cycling tour. I’ll explain the plan, and then you can ask questions. We’ll meet on Saturday morning outside the train [14]station[/14]. Please don’t wait at the sports centre, because the car park there is being repaired. Everyone must bring a [15]helmet[/15]. We can lend bikes, but we don’t have enough safety equipment for everyone. The morning route is quite easy and follows the river until we reach the [16]lake[/16]. We’ll stop there for photos and a short rest. Lunch will be a [17]picnic[/17], so bring something that is easy to carry in a small bag. There is a shop nearby, but it may be closed. In the afternoon, each group will receive a [18]map[/18]. The route is clearly marked, but the map will help if anyone takes a wrong turning. We expect to finish by [19]eight[/19] o’clock in the evening. That sounds late, but there will be plenty of breaks, and we won’t ride fast. If the weather is very wet, I’ll send everyone a message on Friday night. The tour is suitable for people who cycle sometimes, but it is not for complete beginners. Please check your brakes before Saturday, and tell us if your bike needs a quick safety check. We’ll carry basic tools, but we cannot repair every problem on the road. There will be a slower group for people who want to take more photographs. Parents should collect younger riders from the station at the end.  If you have any medicine with you, keep it in your own bag and tell one of the leaders before we start.

questions.explanation: The speaker says they will meet outside the train station.
The speaker says everyone must bring a helmet.
The speaker says the morning route follows the river until they reach the lake.
The speaker says lunch will be a picnic.
The speaker says each group will receive a map.
The speaker says they expect to finish by eight o'clock.
